preamble: A direct shift from the alveolar stop [d] to the labiodental fricative [v] is rare. It typically requires an intermediate stage of spirantization to [ð] (the voiced 'th' sound), followed by 'th-fronting', where the acoustic similarity between [ð] and [v] causes the articulation to move to the lips.

examples.note: The future tense marker derived from the verb 'to see' or 'to give' lenited from a stop to a labiodental fricative.
Sporadic lenition and fronting of the preposition in highly localized dialects.
